$description: Binance — bold Binance Yellow accent on a deep crypto-black canvas, trading-floor urgency, BinanceNova / BinancePlex stack (IBM Plex substitute), tabular numerics. Single accent does all brand voltage; trading-up green / trading-down red are semantic price-direction tokens, never decorative. Multi-theme: dark for marketing/product, light for transactional surfaces — the deck reuses the light palette ONLY for the closing footer-reset band that closes every Binance page. Flat surfaces, no shadows. Derived from getdesign.md/binance/design-md. MIT.

accent.$description: Binance Yellow — the single brand-defining accent. Reserved for primary CTAs, the wordmark, hero / brand-claim headlines, trust-badge numerics. Used scarcely on dark for emphasis. Never decorative. NEVER as body fill.

accent-active.$description: Binance Yellow active / pressed — slightly more saturated yellow.

accent-disabled.$description: Binance Yellow disabled — desaturated dark-yellow on dark canvas.

accent-hover.$description: Alias of accent-active. Per the no-hover policy this is not used in preview output, but kept for theme.xml compatibility.

highlight.$description: Companion bright yellow — small inline highlights, badges, focus rings. Same hex as accent — Binance has one yellow.

black.$description: Body ink — on Binance's dark canvas the default text reads as soft cool white. Semantic-name kept; value flipped to invert the palette without rewriting layouts.

ink.$description: Pure crypto-black — chapter dividers, deepest contrast band beneath the canvas.

graphite.$description: Body strong on dark — pure white for max emphasis on display copy.

steel.$description: Body emphasized on dark — lead text, secondary display.

silver.$description: Muted on dark — sub-headings, breadcrumbs, secondary labels (DESIGN.md `muted`).

fog.$description: Hairline border on dark surfaces — separator strokes, ticker-row dividers (DESIGN.md `hairline-on-dark`). Same hex as surface-card-dark elevated; reads as a brightness-step.

fog-strong.$description: 1px hairline on light footer-reset bands (DESIGN.md `hairline-on-light`). Highest-frequency token on the brand surface.

paper.$description: Elevated card surface — feature cards, content cards, the markets-table-card and trust-badges (DESIGN.md `surface-card-dark`).

paper-2.$description: Strongest elevated surface — emphasized cards, nested cards, hover (DESIGN.md `surface-elevated-dark`). Same hex as fog; the elevation IS the hairline tone.

paper-3.$description: Code-block / terminal background inside dark cards.

off-white.$description: On-dark text — soft cool white that echoes the body ink.

off-white-2.$description: On-dark soft — footer body text, secondary labels.

rule-dark.$description: Hairlines on dark surfaces (alias of fog).

surface-dark.$description: Trading-pane / cover surface — Binance deep crypto-black. Slightly warm tinted, never pure black.

surface-dark-elevated.$description: Elevated cards inside dark bands — markets-table-card, ticker tile, trust badges.

surface-dark-soft.$description: Code block / terminal backgrounds inside dark cards. Slightly lifted from canvas.

surface-soft.$description: Section dividers, soft band backgrounds — never light, Binance is dark-first.

surface-cream-strong.$description: Strongest surface — selected tabs, emphasized bands.

canvas-light.$description: Light footer-reset canvas — used ONLY in the closing-page light band that visually closes every Binance page (DESIGN.md `canvas-light`). Never on body slides.

surface-soft-light.$description: Light footer band surface — the iconic 'marketing reset' that closes every page (DESIGN.md `surface-soft-light`).

ink-on-light.$description: Default text on the light footer-reset band (DESIGN.md `ink` / `body-on-light`).

trading-up.$description: Price-up green — semantic price-direction signal. Used as text colour in tables, charts, inline ticker arrows. NEVER as a button background; NEVER for generic 'success'.

trading-down.$description: Price-down red — semantic price-direction signal. Same usage rules as trading-up; NEVER as a generic 'error'.

accent-teal.$description: Info / focus-ring blue (DESIGN.md `info`). Single-product accent (Smart Money) — treat as a system info colour, not a brand colour.

warning.$description: Status warning — Binance Yellow doubles as caution.

white.$description: Canvas — Binance deep crypto-black, the brand-defining dark backdrop. Semantic-name kept; value flipped to invert the palette without rewriting layouts.

status-current.$description: Status: in progress — alias of severity-medium.

status-next.$description: Status: not yet started — muted neutral.

font-family.$description: Geometric sans pairing — BinanceNova / BinancePlex (custom Binance type) display + body, with IBM Plex Sans as the documented open-source substitute. BinancePlex (or IBM Plex Mono) carries every numeric — prices, volumes, percentages, KPI values, axis ticks. Mixing them is a system violation per DESIGN.md.

font-weight.$description: Display-mega 700 (hero h1 register), display-md/sm 600 (section heads), title 600. Body 400. Button / chip / kpi-key 600. Bold reads as ticker-screen confidence; thin display is OFF-brand — Binance never softens display weight to 400 the way Airtable or Stripe does.

medium.$description: Section heads + display-md — editorial confidence without bombast.

semibold.$description: Default DISPLAY weight for title-lg / title-md / title-sm; CTA labels; chip / nav-link voice.

bold.$description: Hero h1 + display-lg ('FUNDS ARE SAFU' voice) + KPI value (BinancePlex). The trading-platform 'this number must read at a glance' weight.

font-size.$description: Sizes scaled to the 1920×1080 deck baseline. DESIGN.md hierarchy is web-density (12–64px); deck context lifts each step roughly 2-3× while preserving the 700/600/400 hierarchy.

display.$description: Hero h1 register — '316,258,026 USERS TRUST US' / '$4,000,000 USDT' prize pool. Weight 700, BinancePlex on numerics, BinanceNova on copy.

huge.$description: Display-lg — 'FUNDS ARE SAFU' brand-claim voice. Weight 700, tracking -0.02em.

sub.$description: Sub-headline. Weight 400 — the editorial counterpoint to 700.

kpi-value.$description: Trust-badge / hero stat — BinancePlex 700 with negative tracking.

chip.$description: Pill chip / status badge — UPPERCASE 600 1.4px tracked.

ticker-price.$description: Markets-row price cell — BinancePlex 600 with negative tracking.

ticker-pct.$description: Markets-row 24h change cell — BinancePlex 500, coloured green/red.

radius.$description: Binance radius hierarchy is tighter than typical marketing systems — most surfaces sit at 6–12px. The pill (9999) is a deliberate exception, reserved for top-of-page 'this is THE action' CTAs. Same code path that produces BMW's rectangles (radius=0) and Spotify's pills (radius=9999) produces Binance's 6/8/12 register.

sm.$description: Small inline buttons (subscribe, trading-up / trading-down inline).

md.$description: Standard CTA buttons, primary buttons, primary input fields.

lg.$description: Search input, content cards, trust badges, sub-cards.

xl.$description: Elevated card containers (markets-table-card, QR-promo-card, CTA bands).

btn.$description: Standard CTA — sharp-but-not-zero, the design.md primary register.

chip.$description: Small inline chips and trading-up / trading-down inline labels.

card.$description: Elevated content card — markets-table-card, trust badges.

pill.$description: Reserved for prominent feature CTAs (Sign Up, Join Now, Get the App). One pill per layout, max.

layout.$description: Binance pages mix marketing bands with dense product surfaces (markets tables, FAQ accordions). Section spacing is tighter than airy marketing sites — 80px is the right rhythm. Card padding is generous (32px) so trust-badge numerics breathe.

ticker-rows.$description: Markets-table-card carries 5 rows on the cover layout — matches the homepage 5-row ticker.

cover.$description: Binance cover treatment — markets-hero split, dark canvas, half-yellow-headline / half-markets-ticker. Yellow signup-pill at lower-left is the iconic CTA shape.

split-ratio.$description: Left half: hero headline + signup pill. Right half: markets-table-card.

section-marker.$description: Yellow ▌ left-rule — an 8px vertical bar in Binance Yellow set flush to the headline. Replaces BMW's M-stripe role and Spotify's equalizer-marker role at chapter dividers and major editorial breaks. Reads as the trading-pane 'active row' indicator.

photography.$description: Binance photography — lifestyle shots of people-using-the-app cropped to 12px corners on the dark canvas. 3D-rendered coin-stack illustrations and trophy icons sit as full-bleed elements next to display headlines. No duotone, no atmospheric gradients — Binance trusts color-block contrast (yellow / dark) to do the visual separation work.

headline-rule.$description: Binance display register — sentence case (or UPPERCASE for brand-claim moments like FUNDS ARE SAFU), weight 700 on hero, 600 on section heads. Negative tracking on display: trading platforms need numbers and headlines to read at a glance.

chip-rule.$description: Binance pill button / chip / page-meta label — UPPERCASE 13–18px / 600 / 1.4px (~0.1em) tracking. NO terminator (no '›' / '→' on labels). Slightly less tracked than Spotify's 0.1em — Binance is denser.

shadow.$description: Binance is shadow-free, opposite of Spotify. Depth comes from contrast between canvas-dark (#0B0E11) and surface-card-dark (#1E2329) — a 12-step lightness jump that reads as a clear elevation boundary. The single exception is a faint shadow on cards that float over photography — used sparingly on the Lifestyle cover.
